title: Raw-Garlic Burger
description: A raw-friendly, garlicky, and crunchy Venezuelan dish featuring ground beef that is slow-cooked to perfection.
instructions: Chop the garlic cloves and place them in a small mixing bowl.
Add olive oil, lemon juice, chopped parsley, salt, and black pepper to the bowl with the garlic. Mix well until the mixture is evenly distributed among the ingredients.
Transfer the marinade into a blender or food processor and puree until smooth, then set aside for later use in assembling burgers.
In a large mixing bowl, combine ground beef, about half of the prepared marinated sauce, and mix thoroughly with your hands. Be gentle to keep it raw-friendly.
Form the mixture into patties that are slightly smaller than burger buns should be when filled. Place them on a tray lined with paper towels or in a freezer bag for storage if not immediately used.
Wrap each patty tightly with plastic wrap and place them in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour to chill, allowing flavors to meld together more evenly.

To assemble burgers, remove patties from refrigeration. Take one patty, apply some of the remaining marinated sauce on top of it, close over a bun (if using), and serve immediately as a raw-friendly snack.
